title: Deepcharts (Volumetrica) Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees
meta_description: DeepCharts (Volumetrica) 2026 pricing: Free, Pro ($29/mo), Prop Firm ($19/mo). See all plans, features, hidden fees, and how it compares to Bookmap & ATAS.
excerpt: See DeepCharts (Volumetrica) 2026 pricing: Free, Pro, Prop Firm plans, features, hidden fees, and how it compares to Bookmap, ATAS, and Sierra Chart.
content:
Table of Contents

  • DeepCharts (Volumetrica) Overview
  • DeepCharts (Volumetrica) Pricing 2026
  • Quick Comparison: DeepCharts vs. Alternatives
  • Features and Limitations by Plan
  • Hidden Fees and Extra Costs
  • Who Should Use DeepCharts?
  • Final Recommendation for 2026

DeepCharts (Volumetrica) Overview

DeepCharts (also known as Volumetrica DeepCharts) is a browser-based order flow and charting platform that has quickly gained traction among prop firm traders and order flow beginners since its launch in 2022. Unlike legacy platforms, DeepCharts requires no software installation and works seamlessly on any device with a modern browser. Its integration with prop firm data feeds and a genuinely free tier make it a compelling choice for those starting out or trading on funded accounts. However, as highlighted in our DeepCharts (Volumetrica) review, it does have a smaller user base and may introduce some latency compared to native desktop apps, especially for high-frequency scalpers.

DeepCharts (Volumetrica) Pricing Guide 2026

As of 2026, DeepCharts continues to offer a transparent pricing structure with three main plans: Free, Pro, and Prop Firm Integration. Here’s a detailed breakdown of each:

  • Free Plan: $0/month — Ideal for beginners. Includes basic order flow charts, limited historical data, and delayed market data. No credit card required.
  • Pro Plan: $29/month or $299/year — Unlocks advanced charting, full order flow analytics, DOM, unlimited workspaces, and priority support. Real-time data is available for select exchanges (see below for data fees).
  • Prop Firm Integration: $19/month — Designed for funded traders. Connects directly to supported prop firm data feeds (e.g., Topstep, Apex), bypassing exchange data fees. Includes all Pro features.

All plans are browser-based, with no installation or hardware requirements. DeepCharts accepts credit cards and PayPal, and offers a 7-day free trial of the Pro plan for new users.

Quick Comparison: DeepCharts vs. Alternatives

Platform Rating Free Plan Pro Price (Monthly) Prop Firm Support Browser-Based Best For
DeepCharts (Volumetrica) 3.8/5 Yes $29 Yes Yes Order flow beginners, prop firm traders
Bookmap 4.5/5 No $39 Partial No Advanced order flow, desktop users
ATAS 4.2/5 Yes (limited) $69 No No Serious futures traders
Sierra Chart 4.3/5 No $36 No No Customizable, advanced charting

For a more detailed breakdown, see our DeepCharts vs. Bookmap comparison.

Features and Limitations by Plan

Understanding what you get at each price point is crucial. Here’s what’s included in each DeepCharts plan as of 2026:

  • Free Plan:
    • Basic order flow charts (footprint, volume profile)
    • 1 workspace
    • Delayed data (10-15 min)
    • Limited historical data (up to 7 days)
    • No custom indicators or DOM
    • Email support only
  • Pro Plan:
    • All Free features plus:
    • Real-time data (with exchange subscription)
    • Unlimited workspaces and layouts
    • Advanced chart types (delta, imbalance, cluster)
    • DOM (Depth of Market) and trade ladder
    • Custom indicators and scripting (beta)
    • Priority email/chat support
  • Prop Firm Integration:
    • All Pro features
    • Direct connection to supported prop firm data feeds
    • No exchange data fees (uses prop firm’s data)
    • Designed for funded account traders

Note: DeepCharts does not currently support direct brokerage execution; it is primarily a charting and analytics tool. For execution, you’ll need to use your prop firm’s platform or a supported broker.

Hidden Fees and Extra Costs

While DeepCharts is upfront about its subscription pricing, there are a few extra costs to be aware of in 2026:

  • Exchange Data Fees: Real-time data for CME, NYMEX, CBOT, and EUREX requires a separate subscription, typically $5–$15/month per exchange. These fees are paid directly to the exchange and are standard across all platforms.
  • Historical Data: Extended historical data (over 1 year) may require an additional $10/month add-on, depending on your plan and data provider.
  • Custom Indicator Marketplace: Some advanced indicators or third-party scripts may be sold separately in the DeepCharts marketplace (pricing varies, typically $10–$50 one-time).
  • API Access: As of 2026, API access for automation or custom integrations is in beta and may be a paid add-on ($15/month).

There are no hidden cancellation fees, and you can downgrade or cancel at any time from your account dashboard.

Who Should Use DeepCharts?

DeepCharts is best suited for:

  • Order flow beginners who want to learn without upfront costs
  • Prop firm traders who need browser-based analytics and direct data feed integration
  • Traders on the go who value device flexibility and zero installation

If you require ultra-low latency for high-frequency scalping, or need advanced automation, you may prefer a desktop platform like Bookmap or Sierra Chart. For a broader feature set and more mature ecosystem, see our DeepCharts vs. Bookmap comparison.

Final Recommendation for 2026

For 2026, DeepCharts (Volumetrica) stands out as the most accessible browser-based order flow platform, especially for prop firm traders and those new to order flow analytics. Its free tier is unmatched for learning, and the Pro plan remains competitively priced at $29/month. The Prop Firm Integration plan is a unique value proposition, saving traders on exchange data fees and simplifying setup. While it may lack some of the advanced features and speed of established desktop platforms, DeepCharts is rapidly evolving and is an excellent choice for most retail and prop firm traders in 2026.

Ready to try it? Start with the free plan or explore the Pro trial — and check out our full DeepCharts (Volumetrica) review for hands-on insights.
